description: Nine automated classification parameters used to tag each paper in the corpus and drive the taxonomy classifier

CP-01.name: Temporal Sequencing
CP-01.description: Ordering of TTPs into time-stamped kill-chain sequences. Models APT behaviour as a time-ordered Markov chain.

CP-02.name: Behavioral Fingerprinting
CP-02.description: Identification of adversary-unique behavioral signatures across multiple campaigns to enable attribution.

CP-03.name: NLP-Based TTP Extraction
CP-03.description: Automated parsing of unstructured threat intelligence reports, incident documents, and CTI feeds to extract technique mentions.

CP-04.name: Graph-Theoretic Modeling
CP-04.description: Representation of attack campaigns as directed graphs — nodes are techniques, edges are temporal/causal relationships.

CP-05.name: ML-Driven Attribution
CP-05.description: Supervised classification of observed TTP sequences to known APT groups using labeled training data.
CP-05.note: CP-05 is embedded within other parameters in most papers; rarely the sole classification method

CP-06.name: Indicator Correlation
CP-06.description: Cross-campaign clustering and deduplication of Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) to link campaigns to threat actors.

CP-07.name: Anomaly Detection
CP-07.description: Statistical deviation analysis to flag novel techniques, zero-day exploits, and behaviors that deviate from established baselines.

CP-08.name: C2 Traffic Analysis
CP-08.description: Protocol-level and behavioral classification of command-and-control communication patterns in network traffic.

CP-09.name: Exfiltration Modeling
CP-09.description: Volume, timing, and protocol-based classification of data exfiltration events, with focus on low-and-slow transfer detection.
